title: 6 Account Reconciliation Service
body_text:
6	Account Reconciliation Service
6.1	Overview of Account Reconciliation Service
The Account Reconciliation service is intended for multi-branch financial institutions and is available on a subscription basis. The OREC file is used to automatically reconcile your financial institutionâs Canadian dollar, US dollar, Pound Sterling, and Euro accounts at Central 1. Your financial institution uses its own software, or software from a third-party vendor, to run the reconciliation process to compare your banking systemâs data to your Central 1 account activities.
OREC files are created for business days only and are available on the File and Report Exchange on the first day after the date of the transactions (i.e., Tuesday to Saturday). The content of an OREC file includes all transactions posted to your financial institutionâs Central 1 account on the specified date. For further details on file availability, structure, and format, see OREC File Specifications, Form 8951.
Note:	A small number of financial institutions in BC use PREC File Specifications, Form 3750, for automatic reconciliation.
Related Information
OREC File Specifications,
Form 8951
PREC File Specifications (BC Only),
Form 3750
6.2	Setting up the Account Reconciliation Service
Contact Central 1 to request access to the Account Reconciliation service. See Section 1.8, Contacts and Websites.
Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725, for charges associated with this service. Other financial institutions can contact their Central 1 relationship manager.
END
